title: Pleurothallis serpentula
taxonomy: Pleurothallis serpentula is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the Pleurothallis genus. It belongs to the subtribe Pleurothallidinae and is native to Central and South America. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae) and is also known as the 'pleurothallid orchids'. These orchids are typically epiphytes or terrestrial plants that thrive in shaded and humid environments. The classification of Pleurothallis serpentula is primarily based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships.

intro: Pleurothallis serpentula, also known as serpent's tongue orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis serpentula include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat respiratory ailments, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat fever and other infections.
